workflow.fallback: Direct shell-based retrieval of public pages and PDFs
workflow.blocker: Neither `firecrawl` nor `npx firecrawl` was available in the runtime.

targetBuyerPainPoints.statement: Agents and acquisition teams need faster awareness because constrained supply leaves buyers with fewer options and supports higher prices.
Palma and the southwest cannot be treated as one market; buyer guidance depends on corridor-level and neighborhood-level differences.
Cross-border demand remains material, so local teams compete in a market shaped by international buyers, not just domestic demand.
Teams need signal over noise because political headlines about foreign-buyer restrictions can confuse clients even when the legal framework has not changed.

validatedProofPoints.claim: Supply pressure is real, so weekly listing-change visibility is valuable.
support: Spanish Property Insight reported that homes for sale in Spain fell 11% year over year in Q4 2025, citing Idealista as the source.
Knight Frank described Mallorca supply as tight and demand as deep in its 2026 residential market insight.
validatedProofPoints.copyUse: Strong website claim if phrased as a market backdrop rather than a Palma-only statistic.

validatedProofPoints.claim: The southwest corridor remains Mallorca's blue-chip acquisition zone.
support: Knight Frank says the southwest, from Port d'Andratx to Bendinat and Portals, retains blue-chip status.
Knight Frank notes sea-view villas in that corridor can exceed €15 million.
validatedProofPoints.geographyFit: Directly relevant to the stated wedge
validatedProofPoints.copyUse: Strong website claim for audience fit and market focus.

validatedProofPoints.claim: Mallorca remains an internationally competed market, especially in premium segments.
support: Spanish Property Insight summarized the Engel & Volkers H1 2025 update: overseas purchasers accounted for 28% of all Balearic transactions.
Knight Frank reports foreign demand on Mallorca is nearly double 2010 levels and says German and British buyers still dominate while the nationality mix diversifies.
validatedProofPoints.copyUse: Use to explain why acquisition teams need faster, evidence-backed workflows.

validatedProofPoints.claim: The market is resilient rather than distressed, which raises the value of monitoring price moves and scarce opportunities.
support: Spanish Property Insight summarized over 7,300 Balearic home sales in H1 2025, slightly above the ten-year average.
Knight Frank says prime values on Mallorca rose around 3% in the last 12 months and forecasts roughly 2% to 4% growth in 2026.
validatedProofPoints.copyUse: Good support for 'don't miss inventory and price cuts' messaging; avoid implying broad distress.

validatedProofPoints.claim: The current product slice already turns listings into structured supply, pricing, and comparable outputs with audit trails.
support: The local CLI produced normalized listings, municipality medians, cohort summaries, comparable sets, raw-record paths, and SHA-256 audit traces on 2026-03-27.
validatedProofPoints.geographyFit: Internal product proof, not external market proof
validatedProofPoints.copyUse: Use for product-proof screenshots or UI captions, not as a live-coverage claim.

marketExamples.label: Palma signal
marketExamples.summary: Palma benefits from major infrastructure and connectivity investment that supports year-round demand.
details: Knight Frank cites a €550 million airport expansion.
The report says Mallorca has links to 166 destinations served by 52 airlines.
The same source highlights Palma waterfront regeneration and Club de Mar upgrades.

marketExamples.label: Southwest corridor signal
marketExamples.summary: The southwest remains premium and supply-constrained, with pricing well above emerging value pockets elsewhere on the island.
details: Knight Frank says the southwest from Port d'Andratx to Bendinat and Portals remains blue-chip.
Sea-view villas in the southwest can surpass €15 million.
The report contrasts this with east-coast villas around €6 million to €6.5 million, roughly half southwest pricing.

marketExamples.label: Current internal southwest-style output
marketExamples.summary: The internal product slice already produces municipality and comp outputs that can be shown on the website, albeit from a fixture rather than a live feed.
details: The local report generated a Calvia sale-villa cohort median asking price of €2.35 million.
The same run linked a Santa Ponsa villa against Palma comparables with raw-record traceability.

copyCaveats.statement: Do not claim comprehensive live Mallorca coverage yet. The current local product proof is fixture-based, not a live source crawl.
Label Balearic-wide or Spain-wide statistics clearly. Do not present them as Palma-only or southwest-only figures.
Avoid crisis or distress framing. The evidence points to resilient pricing, tight supply, and selective scarcity rather than broad market weakness.
Avoid suggesting foreign-buyer restrictions are active policy. The March 4, 2026 Balearic proposal failed and the legal framework did not change.

sources.title: Mallorca Residential Market Insight 2026
sources.notes: Primary market report PDF with Mallorca-specific prime-market detail.

sources.title: Balearic market update (H1) from Engel & Volkers
sources.publisher: Spanish Property Insight summarizing Engel & Volkers
sources.notes: Secondary summary citing E&V and official data; useful for Balearic transaction and buyer-mix figures.

sources.title: Prices rise as supply of homes for sale shrinks across most areas
sources.notes: Secondary article citing Idealista; strong for national supply-tightness backdrop.

sources.title: Balearic proposal to "ban" non-resident buyers was just attention-grabbing theatrics
sources.notes: Useful policy caveat source; not a core demand or pricing source.

sources.title: Local report-latest output from fixture ingestion
sources.notes: Product proof only. Not a public live-market source.
